rationale: Tool routing must minimise total tool invocations per turn. Each tool call consumes 1 RPM slot from the 32 RPM Free Tier budget. The minimum-tools principle is enforced: use the fewest tools that produce a complete, accurate answer.

github.rationale: GitHub queries only justified when repository-specific content is needed. General programming questions do not require live GitHub access.

brain_memory.rationale: Memory query only justified when user-specific context meaningfully changes the answer.

web_search.rationale: Web search justified only for time-sensitive data. Static knowledge collections are preferred when available.

knowledge_rag.rationale: RAG does not consume NIM RPM. Run before NIM call. Preferred over web search for static domain content.

calculator.rationale: Arithmetic errors in prose are a known LLM failure mode. Calculator eliminates the risk with zero RPM cost.

batching_rule.rationale: Sequential tool → NIM → tool → NIM chains are the most common source of RPM exhaustion and high latency. Batching all tool calls into a single pre-NIM pass reduces RPM cost per turn from N to 1, and eliminates chained wait time.

minimum_tools_principle.enforcement: Before any tool call, evaluate: is the answer already determinable from conversation context alone?
minimum_tools_principle.rationale: Every unnecessary tool call wastes RPM quota, adds latency, and increases token cost. On a 32 RPM Free Tier budget, tool discipline is a hard production requirement, not a preference.
